title: Living in Boulder vlog 6 - 20 - 2017: Collecting Green Screen Props

enrichment.summary: Kyle vlogs on June 20, 2017 from Boulder about his obsessive green screen background collecting, having grown his library from 855 to 1655 backgrounds in a short time. He also shares a creative idea to visualize his book collection as armies of agents facing off against each other.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: deeply engrossed and excited, planning to stop downloading once past 2000
enthusiastic about turning book chapters/categories into competing agent armies as a fun visual project

entities: In Birdland (likely a book or audio)
Church History by Sousum (phonetic; exact title unclear)

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle didn't do a video yesterday because he was too busy with his green screens.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The last time Kyle did a video, he had 855 green screen backgrounds.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle now has 1655 green screen backgrounds and probably has over 2000.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle is not downloading green screens manually but is subscribing to green screen channels and downloading interesting ones.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle plans to organize his green screens into folders, with each folder containing around 30 subfolders.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle is excited to use his green screens for today's video because he has so many prepared.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle has been listening to 'In Birdland' and 'Church History' by Sousum.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle had an idea to create a graph of his book sorting that resembles an agent, with each chapter represented as a person.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle wants to have armies of books square off against each other, such as Greek classics versus Norman classics.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle is considering using a visual tool like Google Super Puzzle to represent his book armies.
